name: frp Auto-Setup (SSH + Tunnel)
author: panel@bytedock.local
description: Automatically SSHes into your DigitalOcean droplet, installs and configures frps, opens the required ports, then starts frpc locally to tunnel your Minecraft server. All you need is your DO droplet IP, SSH credentials, and an auth token.
startup:
#!/bin/bash

apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y -qq curl wget tar openssh-client ufw > /dev/null

FRP_VERSION="{{FRP_VERSION}}"
DO_IP="{{DO_IP}}"
SSH_USER="{{SSH_USER}}"
SSH_PORT="{{SSH_PORT}}"
FRP_TOKEN="{{FRP_TOKEN}}"
FRP_BIND_PORT="{{FRP_BIND_PORT}}"
JAVA_REMOTE_PORT="{{JAVA_REMOTE_PORT}}"
BEDROCK_REMOTE_PORT="{{BEDROCK_REMOTE_PORT}}"
JAVA_LOCAL_PORT="{{JAVA_LOCAL_PORT}}"
BEDROCK_LOCAL_PORT="{{BEDROCK_LOCAL_PORT}}"
LOCAL_IP="{{LOCAL_IP}}"

# ── SSH key setup ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
mkdir -p ~/.ssh && chmod 700 ~/.ssh

if [ ! -f ~/.ssh/do_key ]; then
  if [ -f /data/do_key ]; then
    cp /data/do_key ~/.ssh/do_key
    chmod 600 ~/.ssh/do_key
    echo "[bytedock] SSH key loaded from /data/do_key."
  else
    echo "[bytedock] ERROR: No SSH key found."
    echo "[bytedock]   → Generate a key pair: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f do_key"
    echo "[bytedock]   → Add do_key.pub to your DO droplet: ssh-copy-id -i do_key.pub ${SSH_USER}@${DO_IP}"
    echo "[bytedock]   → Upload the private key (do_key) to this server's /data/ folder."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# Trust the DO host automatically
ssh-keyscan -p "${SSH_PORT}" -H "${DO_IP}" >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null

SSH="ssh -i ~/.ssh/do_key -p ${SSH_PORT} -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ${SSH_USER}@${DO_IP}"

echo "[bytedock] Testing SSH connection to ${DO_IP}..."
if ! $SSH 'echo ok' > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "[bytedock] ERROR: SSH connection failed. Check your DO_IP, SSH_USER, SSH_PORT, and that do_key is uploaded."
  exit 1
fi
echo "[bytedock] SSH connection OK."

# ── Set up frps on the DO droplet ────────────────────────────────
echo "[bytedock] Setting up frps on DO droplet..."
$SSH bash <<ENDSSH
  set -e

  # Install frps if not present
  if [ ! -f /usr/local/bin/frps ]; then
    echo "[do] Downloading frp ${FRP_VERSION}..."
    ARCH=\$(uname -m)
    if [ "\$ARCH" = "aarch64" ] || [ "\$ARCH" = "arm64" ]; then
      FRP_ARCH="arm64"
    else
      FRP_ARCH="amd64"
    fi
    FRP_DIR="frp_${FRP_VERSION}_linux_\${FRP_ARCH}"
    wget -qO /tmp/frp.tar.gz \
      "https://github.com/fatedier/frp/releases/download/v${FRP_VERSION}/\${FRP_DIR}.tar.gz"
    tar -xzf /tmp/frp.tar.gz -C /tmp
    mv /tmp/\${FRP_DIR}/frps /usr/local/bin/frps
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/frps
    rm -rf /tmp/frp.tar.gz /tmp/\${FRP_DIR}
    echo "[do] frps installed."
  else
    echo "[do] frps already installed, skipping download."
  fi

  # Write frps config
  mkdir -p /etc/frp
  cat > /etc/frp/frps.toml <<EOF
bindPort = ${FRP_BIND_PORT}
auth.method = "token"
auth.token = "${FRP_TOKEN}"
EOF
  echo "[do] frps config written."

  # Open ports with ufw if available
  if command -v ufw &> /dev/null; then
    ufw allow ${FRP_BIND_PORT}/tcp > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
    ufw allow ${JAVA_REMOTE_PORT}/tcp > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
    ufw allow ${BEDROCK_REMOTE_PORT}/udp > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
    echo "[do] ufw rules added."
  fi

  # Install systemd service
  cat > /etc/systemd/system/frps.service <<EOF
[Unit]
Description=frp server
After=network.target

[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/frps -c /etc/frp/frps.toml
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

  systemctl daemon-reload
  systemctl enable frps
  systemctl restart frps
  echo "[do] frps service started."
ENDSSH

echo "[bytedock] DO droplet setup complete."

# ── Download frpc locally ────────────────────────────────────────
if [ ! -f frpc ]; then
  echo "[bytedock] Downloading frpc locally..."
  ARCH=$(uname -m)
  if [ "$ARCH" = "aarch64" ] || [ "$ARCH" = "arm64" ]; then
    FRP_ARCH="arm64"
  else
    FRP_ARCH="amd64"
  fi
  FRP_DIR="frp_${FRP_VERSION}_linux_${FRP_ARCH}"
  wget -qO frp.tar.gz \
    "https://github.com/fatedier/frp/releases/download/v${FRP_VERSION}/${FRP_DIR}.tar.gz"
  tar -xzf frp.tar.gz
  mv ${FRP_DIR}/frpc ./frpc
  rm -rf frp.tar.gz ${FRP_DIR}
  chmod +x frpc
  echo "[bytedock] frpc ready."
fi

# ── Write frpc config ────────────────────────────────────────────
cat > frpc.toml <<EOF
serverAddr = "${DO_IP}"
serverPort = ${FRP_BIND_PORT}
auth.method = "token"
auth.token = "${FRP_TOKEN}"

[[proxies]]
name = "mc-java"
type = "tcp"
localIP = "${LOCAL_IP}"
localPort = ${JAVA_LOCAL_PORT}
remotePort = ${JAVA_REMOTE_PORT}

[[proxies]]
name = "mc-bedrock"
type = "udp"
localIP = "${LOCAL_IP}"
localPort = ${BEDROCK_LOCAL_PORT}
remotePort = ${BEDROCK_REMOTE_PORT}
EOF

echo "[bytedock] frpc config written."
echo "[bytedock] Connecting tunnel to ${DO_IP}:${FRP_BIND_PORT}..."
echo "[bytedock] Java:    ${LOCAL_IP}:${JAVA_LOCAL_PORT} → DO:${JAVA_REMOTE_PORT}"
echo "[bytedock] Bedrock: ${LOCAL_IP}:${BEDROCK_LOCAL_PORT} → DO:${BEDROCK_REMOTE_PORT} (UDP)"

exec ./frpc -c frpc.toml

variables.name: DigitalOcean Droplet IP
variables.description: Your DO droplet's public IP address.

variables.name: SSH User
variables.description: SSH username on your DO droplet. Usually 'root' for fresh droplets.

variables.name: SSH Port
variables.description: SSH port on your DO droplet.

variables.name: frp Auth Token
variables.description: A strong secret shared between frps and frpc. Make this unique.

variables.name: frp Control Port
variables.description: Port frps listens on for tunnel connections (must be open on DO firewall).

variables.name: frp Version
variables.description: frp release version. Check https://github.com/fatedier/frp/releases for latest.

variables.name: Local Server IP
variables.description: LAN IP of your Minecraft server. Use 127.0.0.1 if on the same machine as ByteDock.

variables.name: Java Local Port
variables.description: Java Edition port on your local machine.

variables.name: Java Remote Port
variables.description: Java Edition port exposed on your DO droplet.

variables.name: Bedrock Local Port
variables.description: Bedrock Edition port on your local machine (UDP).

variables.name: Bedrock Remote Port
variables.description: Bedrock Edition port exposed on your DO droplet (UDP).
